report_name: Instructure's Canvas Platform Suffers Major Data Breach Attributed to ShinyHunters Hacking Group

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A well-known hacking group that claimed responsibility for the Instructure Canvas LMS breach. The group is known for large-scale data breaches, selling stolen data on dark web forums, and using double-extortion tactics such as defacement and threatening to leak exfiltrated data.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through the threat of leaking stolen student and staff data.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: ShinyHunters gained initial access by exploiting a flaw in the 'Free-For-Teacher' account program, suggesting the use of compromised or maliciously created cloud accounts to get a foothold in the Canvas platform.

attack_patterns.name: Account Manipulation
attack_patterns.description: After gaining initial access, the attackers likely escalated their privileges to gain broader access to the multi-tenant cloud environment, moving from their initial foothold to access data belonging to thousands of other institutions.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The hacking group accessed and exfiltrated large amounts of data, including user PII (names, emails, student IDs) and private messages, from the Canvas cloud storage environment.

attack_patterns.name: Internal Defacement
attack_patterns.description: ShinyHunters altered the web login portals of hundreds of affected schools to display ransom messages as part of their double-extortion strategy.

vulnerabilities.name: Free-For-Teacher Account Program Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: An unspecified vulnerability within the 'Free-For-Teacher' account program on the Instructure Canvas platform was exploited for initial access. The flaw was likely in the registration or authentication process, allowing for account takeover or malicious account creation with elevated access.

identities.name: Instructure
identities.description: The provider of the Canvas learning management system (LMS) that was the primary victim of the data breach.

identities.name: Schools and Universities
identities.description: Thousands of schools, colleges, and universities globally that use the Canvas LMS were affected by the data breach and service disruption.

infrastructure.name: Canvas LMS Cloud Environment
infrastructure.description: The cloud-hosted, multi-tenant environment for the Canvas LMS which was compromised by ShinyHunters to access and exfiltrate data.

infrastructure.name: Defaced School Login Portals
infrastructure.description: The web login portals for various schools using Canvas, which were defaced by ShinyHunters with ransom messages.

indicators.name: Anomalous Free-Tier Cloud Account Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Free-Tier Cloud Account Activity
id: 2b8c1f9d-5a3e-4b1f-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects anomalous activity related to free-tier accounts, such as unusual privilege escalation or cross-tenant access attempts, which could indicate exploitation similar to the Canvas breach vector.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/canvas-system-is-online-after-a-cyberattack-disrupted-thousands-of-schools/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-11
tags:
  - attack.t1078.004
  - attack.t1098
level: medium
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: auditlogs
detection:
  selection:
    OperationName: 'Add member to role'
    InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName|contains: 'freeteacher'
    TargetResources|contains:
      - 'Administrative Units'
      - 'Company Administrator'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects anomalous account creation or privilege escalation from free-tier accounts, which was the initial vector in the Canvas breach.

indicators.name: Web Portal Defacement Detection
indicators.pattern:
title: Web Portal Defacement Keywords
id: 9d0e1f2a-3b4c-5d6e-7f8a-9b0c1d2e3f4a
status: experimental
description: Detects keywords commonly associated with website defacement or ransom messages on web pages.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/canvas-system-is-online-after-a-cyberattack-disrupted-thousands-of-schools/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-11
tags:
  - attack.t1491.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: webserver
detection:
  selection:
    sc-filter-result: 'Matched'
    c-body|contains|all:
      - 'hacked by'
      - 'your data'
      - 'ransom'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects potential website defacement by looking for common attacker messages, similar to how ShinyHunters defaced school login portals.
